mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer purchasing power and construction demand, creating a compounding effect that pushes S&P 500 returns positive over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of co-movement periods, indicating the interaction effect is spurious.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a rising industrial production index signals resilient manufacturing output that offsets higher borrowing costs, leading to short-term positive equity returns as markets price in avoided recession risks.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 70% of tightening episodes, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0, or the threshold shows no statistical edge over a continuous linear model.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a broad-based labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenue forecasts to drive positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The co-movement of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, it signals a contraction in manufacturing and industrial activity that precedes earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO drops below 95.0, with positive outcomes occurring in more than 65% of triggered instances.
